req.title: Bulk import employees — variant 2
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates partial-commit CSV bulk import with per-row error reporting and idempotency. I derived five tests: (1) EP positive — a mixed CSV where valid rows commit and invalid rows surface errors; (2) NEG — uploading a non-CSV file yields HTTP 415; (3) NEG — CSV missing required header columns yields HTTP 422; (4) BVA — a single-data-row CSV (minimum non-empty payload) commits exactly one record; (5) STC — idempotency under retry, uploading the same valid CSV twice leaves the data set unchanged. Together these cover all stated error pathways, the partial-commit logic, and the idempotency variant.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_partial_commit
preconditions: Database is seeded with known-clean employee table
actions: Compose a CSV with 3 valid rows and 2 invalid rows (missing required field)
Capture HTTP status and JSON response body
expected: HTTP 207 Multi-Status (or 200 with body discriminating results)
Response contains 'errors' list with exactly 2 entries
Each error entry references the originating row number
Valid rows are persisted and retrievable via GET /employees
trace: R-WEB-098: error report generated per row
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session, base_url, clean_db

VALID_ROWS = (
    "first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
    "Alice,Smith,alice@example.com,Engineering\n"
    "Bob,Jones,bob@example.com,HR\n"
    "Carol,White,carol@example.com,Finance\n"
)
INVALID_ROWS = (
    ",MissingFirst,bad1@example.com,IT\n"
    "Dave,,bad2@example.com,IT\n"
)

def test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_partial_commit(hr_admin_session, base_url, clean_db):
    csv_content = VALID_ROWS + INVALID_ROWS
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_content.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 3
    assert len(body["errors"]) == 2
    row_nums = {e["row"] for e in body["errors"]}
    assert row_nums == {4, 5}
    employees = hr_admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/employees").json()
    emails = {e["email"] for e in employees}
    assert {"alice@example.com", "bob@example.com", "carol@example.com"}.issubset(emails)

tests.name: test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415
actions: Compose a payload that is a plain text file with .txt extension
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import with MIME type text/plain
expected: Response body indicates only CSV is accepted
trace: R-WEB-098: error pathway — 415 when file not CSV
tests.executable:
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session, base_url

def test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415(hr_admin_session, base_url):
    txt_content = b"first_name last_name email department\nAlice Smith a@b.com Eng"
    files = {"file": ("employees.txt", io.BytesIO(txt_content), "text/plain")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.json()
    assert "csv" in body.get("detail", "").lower() or "unsupported" in body.get("detail", "").lower()

tests.name: test_bulk_import_missing_required_header_returns_422
actions: Compose a CSV whose header row omits the required 'email' column
expected: Response body identifies the missing header column by name
trace: R-WEB-098: error pathway — 422 when header missing required columns
tests.executable:
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session, base_url

BAD_HEADER_CSV = (
    "first_name,last_name,department\n"
    "Alice,Smith,Engineering\n"
)

def test_bulk_import_missing_required_header_returns_422(hr_admin_session, base_url):
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(BAD_HEADER_CSV.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    detail = str(body.get("detail", "")).lower()
    assert "email" in detail

tests.name: test_bulk_import_single_data_row_minimum_boundary
preconditions: Database is seeded with known-clean employee table
actions: Compose a CSV with a valid header and exactly one valid data row (minimum non-empty payload)
Verify the single employee is persisted
trace: R-WEB-098: boundary — minimum data payload (1 row)
tests.executable:
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session, base_url, clean_db

SINGLE_ROW_CSV = (
    "first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
    "Eve,Brown,eve@example.com,Legal\n"
)

def test_bulk_import_single_data_row_minimum_boundary(hr_admin_session, base_url, clean_db):
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(SINGLE_ROW_CSV.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{base_url}/employees/import", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 1
    assert body["errors"] == []
    employees = hr_admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/employees").json()
    emails = [e["email"] for e in employees]
    assert "eve@example.com" in emails

tests.name: test_bulk_import_idempotent_on_retry
preconditions: Database is seeded with known-clean employee table
actions: POST the identical CSV to /employees/import a second time (retry)
expected: Both POST calls return HTTP 200 or 207
Employee count after retry equals employee count after first upload
No duplicate employee records created for the same email
imported_count on second call reflects idempotent behaviour (0 new or deduplicated)
trace: R-WEB-098: action must be idempotent under retry
tests.executable:
import io
from <fixture> import hr_admin_session, base_url, clean_db

IDEMPOTENT_CSV = (
    "first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
    "Frank,Green,frank@example.com,Sales\n"
    "Grace,Hall,grace@example.com,Marketing\n"
)

def _do_import(session, url, csv_bytes):
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_bytes), "text/csv")}
    return session.post(f"{url}/employees/import", files=files)

def test_bulk_import_idempotent_on_retry(hr_admin_session, base_url, clean_db):
    csv_bytes = IDEMPOTENT_CSV.encode()
    r1 = _do_import(hr_admin_session, base_url, csv_bytes)
    assert r1.status_code in (200, 207)
    employees_after_first = hr_admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/employees").json()
    count_after_first = len(employees_after_first)
    r2 = _do_import(hr_admin_session, base_url, csv_bytes)
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 207)
    employees_after_retry = hr_admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/employees").json()
    count_after_retry = len(employees_after_retry)
    assert count_after_retry == count_after_first
    emails = [e["email"] for e in employees_after_retry]
    assert emails.count("frank@example.com") == 1
    assert emails.count("grace@example.com") == 1
